datasets.name: kpi_overall
queryLines: SELECT   ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN compr_freq_act_hz > 0 THEN bt17_suction_c - bt16_evap_c END), 1) AS avg_superheat_k,   ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN compr_freq_act_hz > 0 AND (bt17_suction_c - bt16_evap_c) < 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN compr_freq_act_hz > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0), 1) AS neg_superheat_pct,   ROUND(MAX(bp4_hp_bar), 1) AS max_hp_bar,   ROUND(MAX(bt14_discharge_c), 1) AS max_discharge_c,   ROUND(SUM(tot_int_add_kw * 5.0 / 3600), 1) AS total_heater_kwh,   ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN compr_freq_act_hz > 0 AND priority_mode = 30 THEN bt25_supply_c - bt71_return_c END), 1) AS avg_delta_t_k,   ROUND(AVG(degree_minutes), 0) AS avg_dm,   ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN alarm_active THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 1) AS alarm_pct,   ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN compr_freq_act_hz > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 1) AS compr_duty_pct FROM dev_silver.nibe.silver_nibe_logs

datasets.name: daily_extended
queryLines: SELECT   log_date_parsed AS log_date,   MIN(bt1_outdoor_temp_c) AS min_outdoor_c,   MAX(bt1_outdoor_temp_c) AS max_outdoor_c,   ROUND(AVG(bt1_outdoor_temp_c), 1) AS avg_outdoor_c,   ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN compr_freq_act_hz > 0 AND priority_mode = 30 THEN bt25_supply_c - bt71_return_c END), 1) AS avg_delta_t_k,   ROUND(MAX(CASE WHEN compr_freq_act_hz > 0 THEN bt25_supply_c - bt71_return_c END), 1) AS max_delta_t_k,   ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN priority_mode = 30 THEN calc_supply_c END), 1) AS avg_calc_supply_c,   ROUND(AVG(degree_minutes), 0) AS avg_dm,   MIN(degree_minutes) AS min_dm,   ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN compr_freq_act_hz > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 1) AS compr_duty_pct,   ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN compr_freq_act_hz > 0 THEN compr_freq_act_hz END), 0) AS avg_freq_hz,   ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN tot_int_add_kw > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 1) AS heater_duty_pct,   ROUND(SUM(tot_int_add_kw * 5.0 / 3600), 1) AS heater_energy_kwh,   MAX(tot_int_add_kw) AS heater_max_kw,   ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN compr_freq_act_hz > 0 THEN bt17_suction_c - bt16_evap_c END), 1) AS avg_superheat_k,   ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN compr_freq_act_hz > 0 AND (bt17_suction_c - bt16_evap_c) < 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN compr_freq_act_hz > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0), 1) AS negative_superheat_pct,   ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN compr_freq_act_hz > 0 THEN bp4_hp_bar END), 1) AS avg_hp_bar,   ROUND(MAX(bp4_hp_bar), 1) AS max_hp_bar,   ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN compr_freq_act_hz > 0 THEN bt14_discharge_c END), 1) AS avg_discharge_c,   SUM(CASE WHEN alarm_active THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS alarm_samples,   ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN alarm_active THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 1) AS alarm_pct,   ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN priority_mode = 30 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 0) AS co_pct,   COUNT(*) AS total_samples FROM dev_silver.nibe.silver_nibe_logs GROUP BY log_date_parsed ORDER BY log_date_parsed

datasets.name: superheat_5min
queryLines: SELECT   TIMESTAMP_SECONDS(FLOOR(UNIX_TIMESTAMP(logged_at) / 300) * 300) AS ts_5min,   ROUND(AVG(bt17_suction_c - bt16_evap_c), 1) AS avg_superheat_k,   ROUND(AVG(bp4_hp_bar), 1) AS avg_hp_bar,   ROUND(AVG(bt14_discharge_c), 1) AS avg_discharge_c FROM dev_silver.nibe.silver_nibe_logs WHERE compr_freq_act_hz > 0 GROUP BY TIMESTAMP_SECONDS(FLOOR(UNIX_TIMESTAMP(logged_at) / 300) * 300) ORDER BY ts_5min

datasets.name: heating_curve
queryLines: SELECT   ROUND(bt1_outdoor_temp_c, 0) AS bt1_rounded,   ROUND(AVG(calc_supply_c), 1) AS avg_calc_supply,   ROUND(AVG(bt25_supply_c), 1) AS avg_actual_supply,   ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN tot_int_add_kw > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 0) AS heater_pct,   COUNT(*) AS samples FROM dev_silver.nibe.silver_nibe_logs WHERE compr_freq_act_hz > 0 AND priority_mode = 30 GROUP BY ROUND(bt1_outdoor_temp_c, 0) HAVING COUNT(*) > 50 ORDER BY bt1_rounded

datasets.name: flow_quality
queryLines: SELECT   CASE     WHEN compr_freq_act_hz BETWEEN 20 AND 50 THEN '20-50 Hz'     WHEN compr_freq_act_hz BETWEEN 50 AND 80 THEN '50-80 Hz'     WHEN compr_freq_act_hz BETWEEN 80 AND 110 THEN '80-110 Hz'     WHEN compr_freq_act_hz > 110 THEN '110+ Hz'   END AS freq_band,   ROUND(AVG(bt25_supply_c - bt71_return_c), 1) AS avg_delta_t_k,   ROUND(MAX(bt25_supply_c - bt71_return_c), 1) AS max_delta_t_k,   ROUND(AVG(bt17_suction_c - bt16_evap_c), 1) AS avg_superheat_k,   COUNT(*) AS samples FROM dev_silver.nibe.silver_nibe_logs WHERE compr_freq_act_hz > 0 AND priority_mode = 30 GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

datasets.name: alarm_detail
queryLines: SELECT   alarm_number,   CASE alarm_number     WHEN 183 THEN '183: HP Soft Alarm'     WHEN 271 THEN '271: Low Temp Stop'     WHEN 163 THEN '163: HP Trip'     WHEN 162 THEN '162: HP Trip Max'     ELSE CAST(alarm_number AS STRING)   END AS alarm_label,   COUNT(*) AS samples,   ROUND(COUNT(*) * 5.0 / 3600, 1) AS duration_hours,   ROUND(AVG(bt1_outdoor_temp_c), 1) AS avg_outdoor_c,   ROUND(AVG(compr_freq_act_hz), 0) AS avg_freq_hz,   ROUND(AVG(bp4_hp_bar), 1) AS avg_hp_bar FROM dev_silver.nibe.silver_nibe_logs WHERE alarm_active GROUP BY alarm_number ORDER BY samples DESC

datasets.name: hourly_alarm
queryLines: SELECT   HOUR(logged_at) AS hour_of_day,   CASE alarm_number     WHEN 183 THEN '183: HP Soft'     WHEN 271 THEN '271: Low Temp'     WHEN 163 THEN '163: HP Trip'     WHEN 162 THEN '162: HP Trip Max'     ELSE CAST(alarm_number AS STRING)   END AS alarm_label,   COUNT(*) AS alarm_samples FROM dev_silver.nibe.silver_nibe_logs WHERE alarm_active GROUP BY HOUR(logged_at), alarm_number ORDER BY hour_of_day

datasets.name: subcooling_daily
queryLines: SELECT log_date, subcooling_avg_k, subcooling_min_k, outdoor_avg_c FROM dev_gold.nibe.gold_nibe_daily_summary ORDER BY log_date

datasets.name: defrost_daily
queryLines: SELECT log_date, COUNT(*) AS defrost_count, ROUND(AVG(duration_min), 1) AS avg_duration_min, ROUND(MAX(duration_min), 1) AS max_duration_min, ROUND(AVG(outdoor_avg_c), 1) AS outdoor_avg_c, ROUND(AVG(bt16_end_c), 1) AS avg_bt16_end_c FROM dev_gold.nibe.gold_nibe_defrost_cycles GROUP BY log_date ORDER BY log_date

datasets.name: dhw_daily
queryLines: SELECT log_date, COUNT(*) AS dhw_cycle_count, ROUND(AVG(duration_min), 1) AS avg_duration_min, ROUND(MAX(duration_min), 1) AS max_duration_min, ROUND(AVG(bt6_end_c), 1) AS avg_bt6_end_c, ROUND(MIN(bt6_end_c), 1) AS min_bt6_end_c, ROUND(AVG(heater_duty_pct), 0) AS avg_heater_pct FROM dev_gold.nibe.gold_nibe_dhw_cycles GROUP BY log_date ORDER BY log_date

lines: **Alarm Code Reference** | **183 - EB101 High Pressure Soft Alarm** (17.2h, avg -7.6C outdoor): Compressor running below min frequency due to high-side pressure. Cyclical pattern; soft alarm, system keeps running. **271 - Low Temperature Stop** (15.6h, avg -20.7C outdoor): Compressor stopped, outdoor temp too cold for safe operation. Low-temperature protection cutout. **163 - High Pressure Trip** (0.2h, ~32 bar): Hard alarm, compressor trip at high pressure under high load. **162 - High Pressure Trip Max** (<0.1h, 36.8 bar): Hard alarm, extreme pressure trip. Root cause 162/163: insufficient flow -> high DeltaT -> high condensing pressure.

pages.displayName: Guide — How to Read This Dashboard

lines: ## Guide — What Am I Looking At?
Metric definitions, good/warning/critical thresholds, and current system assessment for this dataset
### Flow and Heat Demand Metrics

**DeltaT Supply-Return (K)** — Temperature difference between heating circuit supply (BT25) and return (BT71). The most important flow indicator. Good: 3-5K | Warning: 5-7K | Critical: >7K causes HP pressure alarms. Current avg: 4.5K (acceptable) but rises to 6.4K at max compressor load — this is the primary trigger for alarms 183 and 163. Typical fix: bleed air from all radiators and underfloor circuits, increase pump GP12 speed.

**Degree Minutes (DM)** — NIBE integrating heat demand controller. Starts at 0, grows negative as the building cools below setpoint. Compressor starts at approx -120 DM. Good: avg > -200 (demand met quickly) | Warning: -200 to -400 | Critical: <-400 stuck = heat pump cannot keep up with demand. Current avg: -370 (critical). System runs at maximum frequency but cannot maintain temperature. Backup electric heater bridges the gap at 3x the cost.
### Refrigerant Circuit Metrics

**Suction Superheat BT17-BT16 (K)** — Temperature margin of refrigerant vapor above evaporation point. Protects compressor from liquid refrigerant entering the cylinder (flooding). Good: 3-7K | Warning: <3K (near flooding) or >15K (undercharged) | Critical: <0K liquid enters compressor. Current avg: 5.9K (OK in average) but 28.8% of compressor running time is negative. This is critical — sustained liquid flooding causes accelerated wear. Action: HVAC engineer should inspect expansion valve calibration and refrigerant charge level.

**HP Pressure BP4 (bar)** — High-side refrigerant pressure. Good: 15-25 bar | Warning: 25-28 bar | Critical: >28 bar triggers soft alarm 183 (freq reduction), >32 bar trips alarm 163 (compressor stops), >36 bar trips alarm 162. Max recorded: 37.9 bar (critical). Root cause: high DeltaT limits heat transfer, forcing pressure up. Fix DeltaT first. **Discharge Temp BT14 (C)** — Compressor outlet gas temp. Good: <80C | Warning: 80-100C | Critical: >100C. Max: 106.8C. Consequence of high-pressure operation. **Heater kWh** — Backup element uses 3-9 kW. Target 0 kWh/day. Total 389.6 kWh = approx EUR 78 vs EUR 26 at COP 3.
### Current System Assessment and Action Plan

**Priority Action Plan (Jan 12-18 data)** | **(1) FIX WATER FLOW — Highest impact, zero cost.** Bleed air from every radiator, underfloor heating manifold, and the heat pump itself. Check circulation pump GP12 is at maximum speed. Check balancing valves are not restricting flow. Target: reduce DeltaT from 6.4K to 3-4K at full load. Expected result: HP pressure drops below 28 bar, alarms 183/163/162 disappear, negative superheat likely improves too. | **(2) REFRIGERANT INSPECTION — Book HVAC engineer.** 28.8% negative superheat over 7 days is abnormal and risks compressor damage. Engineer should measure expansion valve superheat setting (target 5-8K), check refrigerant charge level. | **(3) CAPACITY — Manage expectations.** Degree Minutes at -370 means the heat pump is correctly sized for mild weather but struggles below -15C. Options: improve building insulation, slightly raise heating curve setpoint, or accept electric heater as peak-load supplement for extreme cold. The 7-day heater bill was approx EUR 78 vs EUR 26 if COP 3 achieved — improving flow (step 1) will cut most of that waste.

pages.name: guide

lines:
## Defrost & DHW Analysis

Defrost removes ice from the outdoor coil — the #1 air-source performance issue in cold climates. DHW cycles heat the domestic hot water tank.

**Defrost thresholds:** 5–15 cycles/day normal · avg duration 3–8 min · >20 cycles/day or >10 min avg = investigate
**DHW thresholds:** 1–3 cycles/day · 20–45 min each · end BT6 ≥ 48°C · heater during DHW above −5°C outdoor = unusual
**Subcooling:** Saturation temp − BT15 liquid line. Target 3–8 K. <2 K = undercharge. >10 K = overcharge or condenser fouling.

frame.title: DHW Tank End Temperature BT6 (°C) — Target ≥ 48°C
Condenser Subcooling (K) — Target 3–8 K · <2 K = undercharge · >10 K = overcharge/fouling
